# Patches to the vendored `gdal` 0.19 crate

The upstream `gdal` 0.19 crate does not compile against **libgdal 3.13**
(released 2026-06). We vendor **only the high-level `gdal` crate** here and patch
its source so the `bindgen` feature (which regenerates FFI bindings from the
installed libgdal 3.13 headers) produces a working build.

`gdal-sys` is **not** vendored — it is unpatched and resolved straight from
crates.io, where it builds fine because its bindings are generated by bindgen at
build time (no source changes needed for 3.13).

## Patch 1 — `GDT_Byte` was renamed to `GDT_UInt8` in libgdal 3.13

GDAL 3.13 renamed the `GDT_Byte` C enum value to `GDT_UInt8` (the generated
binding doc literally says *"8-bit unsigned integer (GDT_Byte in GDAL < 3.13)"*).
All references in `src/**/*.rs` were updated:

```diff
-    UInt8 = GDALDataType::GDT_Byte,
+    UInt8 = GDALDataType::GDT_UInt8,
-            GDT_Byte => Ok(GdalDataType::UInt8),
+            GDT_UInt8 => Ok(GdalDataType::UInt8),
-            GDT_Byte | GDT_UInt16 | GDT_Int16 ... =>
+            GDT_UInt8 | GDT_UInt16 | GDT_Int16 ... =>
```

(6 occurrences across `raster/types.rs` and `raster/mdarray.rs`.)

## Patch 2 — `GDALRasterIOExtraArg` gained `bOperateInBufType` (GDAL ≥ 3.13)

`src/raster/rasterband.rs` constructs a `GDALRasterIOExtraArg` struct literal;
libgdal 3.13 added a trailing field `bOperateInBufType: c_int` (defaults to TRUE
in C, we set 0). Added to the `From<RasterIOExtraArg>` initializer:

```diff
             bUseOnlyThisScale: if b_use_only_this_scale { 1 } else { 0 },
+            bOperateInBufType: 0,
         }
```

## Build requirements (libgdal 3.13)

```sh
export PKG_CONFIG_PATH="$(brew --prefix gdal)/lib/pkgconfig"  # gdal.pc
export LIBCLANG_PATH="$(brew --prefix llvm)/lib"              # bindgen needs libclang
cargo build --release
```

The `bindgen` feature (enabled in the root `Cargo.toml`) regenerates the
`gdal-sys` bindings from your installed libgdal headers, so the upstream
`gdal-sys` crate is used unmodified — no prebuilt bindings are needed and the
entire `gdal-sys` source tree is fetched from crates.io rather than vendored.

When an upstream `gdal`-crate release supports libgdal 3.13, drop the
`[patch.crates-io]` line in the root `Cargo.toml` and delete this `vendor/`
directory.

## Runtime requirement

The extension dynamically links `libgdal.so` (and its transitive deps
`libproj.so`, `libsqlite3.so`, …). Set `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` (or install libgdal
system-wide) before `LOAD`. Our *own* PROJ (for `ST_Transform`) is bundled and
statically linked, but GDAL brings its own dynamic PROJ regardless.
